title: The One with Joey's New Brain

jokes.text: It's just... Monica said, 'wedding.'
jokes.setup: Ross is asked what's wrong when he looks uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: He admits the mere word 'wedding' is triggering him
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ross's neurosis about his failed marriages makes this relatable and specific to him

jokes.text: Do you think you could get through a poem? / A short one.
jokes.setup: Monica questions if Ross can handle reading a poem at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Ross's qualifier 'A short one' reveals his own doubts about his emotional stability
jokes.explanation: Efficient character comedy - Ross's self-awareness about his emotional fragility

jokes.text: Whatever it is, I hope it involves winking.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions he's keeping something 'on the q.t.' (quiet/secret)
jokes.punchline: Chandler mocks the outdated expression with sarcastic hope for more old-fashioned behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - sharp, witty observation delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: You're not gonna believe it. My character is coming out of his coma!
jokes.setup: Joey excitedly announces career news
jokes.punchline: The 'great news' is emerging from a coma, highlighting the absurdity of soap opera plots
jokes.explanation: Sets up the soap opera absurdity theme but is more setup than punchline

jokes.text: So great things are happening at work and in your personal life.
jokes.setup: Joey mentions getting a new brain
jokes.punchline: Ross treats a brain transplant as a personal life improvement
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - Ross's deadpan treatment of the absurd as mundane self-improvement

jokes.text: It's ridiculous that you haven't had sex in three and a half months.
jokes.setup: Ross calls brain transplants ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Joey deflects by calling Ross's dry spell ridiculous instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and classic Joey priorities - deflects criticism by attacking Ross's love life

jokes.text: It's winter. There are fewer people on the street.
jokes.setup: Ross needs to explain his dating drought
jokes.punchline: He blames seasonal pedestrian traffic patterns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ross logic - treating dating like a statistical sampling problem with environmental variables

jokes.text: I don't think I've ever seen her finish a beverage.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jessica Lockhart throwing drinks in faces
jokes.punchline: Monica's detail-oriented observation about the soap trope
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica attention to detail applied to soap opera absurdity

jokes.text: Why is this so hard for you to get? I thought you were a scientist.
jokes.setup: Ross struggles to understand soap opera brain transplant logic
jokes.punchline: Joey acts like Ross's scientific knowledge should help him accept fictional nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely backwards logic delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Mainly because he's kissing that other guy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks why Rachel thinks a guy is gay
jokes.punchline: The most obvious possible evidence
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/punchline with good economy but predictable

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, he's too cute to be straight.
jokes.setup: Phoebe points out a different guy
jokes.punchline: Rachel applies shallow stereotyping logic
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotype humor without much clever twist

jokes.text: Knockers will help us figure it out.
jokes.setup: They're trying to determine if a guy is straight or gay
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggests using an obvious test method
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's blunt approach is in character but the humor is fairly juvenile

jokes.text: Or we could use it to call China, see how those guys are doing.
jokes.setup: They could turn the phone into lost and found
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggests using someone's phone for international calls as if checking on friends
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with good economy but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Kind of like a fairy tale for the digital age.
jokes.setup: Rachel fantasizes about meeting the phone's owner
jokes.punchline: She romanticizes what's basically theft into a modern love story
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Rachel's tendency to romanticize situations

jokes.text: That wasn't a date. That was friends getting together and having sex.
jokes.setup: Rachel points out Phoebe had a date three days ago
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual distinction between dating and casual sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - matter-of-fact delivery of something most people would find significant

jokes.text: Yeah, and until now, I didn't think I would love again.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Rachel broke up with Tag a week ago
jokes.punchline: Rachel dramatically claims she thought she'd never love again after one week
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Rachel's dramatic tendency applied to a very short timeframe

jokes.text: Aha! Too slow! / Aha! Too cocky.
jokes.setup: They decide to fairly determine who gets the phone
jokes.punchline: They immediately resort to grabbing for it, with Phoebe countering Rachel's victory
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with escalating competitiveness

jokes.text: What are you going to do, kill him? Like you did Charles? / That was an accident. / And so were you.
jokes.setup: Soap opera dialogue about killing someone
jokes.punchline: Escalates from accidental murder to calling someone an accident
jokes.explanation: Classic soap opera melodrama with good escalation structure

jokes.text: Can I get some ice here?!
jokes.setup: Discussion of how real the slap looked
jokes.punchline: Joey's pained request reveals the slap was actually real
jokes.explanation: Good reveal joke with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Oh, that was a real person?
jokes.setup: Joey introduces himself as the guy from yesterday's scene
jokes.punchline: Cecilia didn't realize Joey was a real actor, thought he was just a prop
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing diva actress obliviousness

jokes.text: Which writer? Was it bald or was it tall?
jokes.setup: Cecilia wants to know which writer told Joey about her departure
jokes.punchline: She categorizes the writers by basic physical features
jokes.explanation: Shows character's casual dismissiveness toward the writers

jokes.text: Fine. But I bet you'd be singing a different tune if we were fighting over a ratchet.
jokes.setup: Rachel rejects Phoebe's name-closeness suggestion for deciding who gets the phone
jokes.punchline: Phoebe points out Rachel would want the name game if it favored her (Rachel/ratchet)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good logical structure showing Phoebe's surprisingly sharp reasoning

jokes.text: No way. You can't use that to get the cute guy and the last blueberry muffin.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions her mom's suicide
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals Phoebe has used this tragedy for trivial gains before
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - reveals Phoebe cynically exploits her trauma for minor advantages

jokes.text: Oh, all right. Oh, so close. If only it said, 'yogurt.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe claims yoga connection when they find 'Yoga' in contacts
jokes.punchline: Rachel fake-consolingly wishes it was yogurt (closer to Rachel)
jokes.explanation: Competent wordplay joke showing competitive dynamic

jokes.text: His new girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks who has Barneys on speed dial
jokes.punchline: Rachel confidently claims it will be her
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's confidence and shopping-focused identity

jokes.text: I think it's the Dying Cat Parade.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what the bagpipe noise is
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic description of the awful sound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - vivid, sarcastic imagery delivered with characteristic wit

jokes.text: Why is your family Ross?
jokes.setup: Ross complains 'Why is your family Scottish?'
jokes.punchline: Monica turns it back on him with the implication that Ross himself is the problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal with perfect economy - Monica's exasperation with her brother distilled into one line

jokes.text: It'll be like when he rapped at my bat mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Monica worries about Ross ruining the wedding
jokes.punchline: References a specific past embarrassment that perfectly establishes Ross's pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent character history joke that makes Ross's current musical ambitions even funnier

jokes.text: I think it's 'Celebration' by Kool and the Gang.
jokes.setup: They listen to Ross's terrible bagpipe playing
jokes.punchline: Chandler claims to identify a recognizable song in the noise
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - finding pattern in chaos

jokes.text: But while you dial, let me show you the features of my new ringing handbag.
jokes.setup: Rachel is about to call the phone to prove Phoebe doesn't have it
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's absurd attempt to explain away the ringing that's about to come from her purse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - desperately improvised excuse that's more absurd than the truth

jokes.text: Yes, she is. Just one moment, please. It's for me!
jokes.setup: Phoebe answers the phone pretending to be someone else when asked for Rachel
jokes.punchline: She transfers the call to herself
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Phoebe's deception with perfect commitment to the bit

jokes.text: You know, to think about my mom and her suicide. / Oh, Phoebe! / What? That's the first time today.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wants to be alone when the guy comes over
jokes.punchline: She again uses her mom's suicide, then reveals it's actually the first time today she's exploited it
jokes.explanation: Good callback with dark comedy twist - Phoebe's casual relationship with exploiting tragedy

jokes.text: I'm 16. / See you in 2003.
jokes.setup: Joey is hitting on someone who reveals they're underage
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately calculates when they'll be legal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect economy but potentially problematic territory

jokes.text: I can do an English accent?
jokes.setup: Cecilia points out Jessica doesn't have an English accent
jokes.punchline: Joey is surprised to learn he was doing one
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness - doesn't even realize his own accent work

jokes.text: That baby's going on my résumé.
jokes.setup: Joey discovers he can do an English accent
jokes.punchline: He immediately wants to add this accidental skill to his résumé
jokes.explanation: Great Joey moment - his pathetic eagerness to pad his résumé with anything

jokes.text: Vikings.
jokes.setup: Monica explains her family was run out of Scotland
jokes.punchline: By Vikings (anachronistic and absurd)
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with historical anachronism for comic effect

jokes.text: Sitting down and being quiet.
jokes.setup: Ross asks what Swedish people are famous for
jokes.punchline: Monica's pointed suggestion that describes ideal wedding behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - Monica's passive-aggressive hint disguised as cultural observation

jokes.text: Because we hate them.
jokes.setup: Ross asks why bagpipes aren't appropriate for the wedding
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt, unvarnished honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and honesty - cuts through all pretense

jokes.text: I'll bet there'll be a wee bit of celebration.
jokes.setup: Ross promises to play something that will change their minds
jokes.punchline: His Scottish-themed pun reveals he's playing 'Celebration'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun with callback structure, very Ross-like dorky wordplay

jokes.text: It's a little weird, but it's getting better.
jokes.setup: Joey attempts to embody Jessica's confidence
jokes.punchline: Cecilia's diplomatic but devastating assessment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from polite but obvious criticism

jokes.text: Probably should've just left years ago when the offers were pouring in... but I just got so comfy here and... I turned down some amazing work. Like what? Well, let's just say if I'd left 15 years ago... the landscape of Mexican cinema would be very different today.
jokes.setup: Cecilia talks about missed opportunities
jokes.punchline: Her grandiose claim about Mexican cinema reveals delusions of importance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing soap star's inflated self-importance with perfect escalation

jokes.text: But I'm also thinking, 'She is hot.'
jokes.setup: Joey compliments Cecilia's acting
jokes.punchline: He adds an inappropriate personal comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey inappropriate honesty but fairly predictable

jokes.text: No, it's because that way the camera only sees her.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Jessica puts hands on faces because she's passionate
jokes.punchline: Cecilia reveals it's actually a selfish camera trick
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation - shows the calculated nature behind what seems romantic

jokes.text: Wow, how long were we arguing for?
jokes.setup: They open the door expecting one person
jokes.punchline: An older man is there instead, implying they argued so long he aged
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the passage of time

jokes.text: We were waiting for a hot guy, and then an even hotter one shows up.
jokes.setup: They're disappointed about the wrong person showing up
jokes.punchline: Rachel reframes it as an upgrade
jokes.explanation: Competent escalation showing Rachel's opportunistic nature

jokes.text: No, they do, but you just have to wait.
jokes.setup: Rachel says they don't make them like that anymore
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact correction about aging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - literal interpretation of figurative language

jokes.text: Actually, I rent the whole place. I just got what you meant. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Cecilia says Joey owns the room
jokes.punchline: Joey first takes it literally about real estate, then gets the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey literal interpretation followed by delayed comprehension

jokes.text: That explains all the women's underwear. / Sure.
jokes.setup: Monica is identified as Joey's roommate
jokes.punchline: Cecilia assumes this explains women's underwear, Joey goes along with it
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Joey's eagerness to go along with assumptions

jokes.text: I'd love to, but my lawyer said I can't do that anymore.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks Cecilia to slap her
jokes.punchline: Cecilia has legal restrictions on slapping fans
jokes.explanation: Excellent character joke implying a history of assault lawsuits from overzealous fan interactions

jokes.text: You're a stupid bitch. / I really can't slap you.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries different approaches to get slapped
jokes.punchline: Even when insulted, Cecilia maintains professional boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the slapping gag with cringe element

jokes.text: After this, she's not gonna be crazy about electricity either.
jokes.setup: Cecilia says Jessica hates horses
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the electric fence will create a new phobia
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about character development

jokes.text: I can imagine you in a short plaid skirt and knee socks.
jokes.setup: Ross asks them to imagine him in a kilt
jokes.punchline: Chandler sexualizes it inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler inappropriate joke but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No. / No.
jokes.setup: Ross finishes his bagpipe performance and asks 'So?'
jokes.punchline: Monica and Chandler's simultaneous, flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy - the simultaneous rejection speaks volumes

jokes.text: Don't you recognize your own... mother?
jokes.setup: Character asks who Joey is in the soap scene
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous brain transplant reveal with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Pays off the soap opera absurdity setup with appropriately melodramatic delivery

jokes.text: The airport? / No, that's LaGuardia.
jokes.setup: Cecilia mentions a film in Guadalajara
jokes.punchline: Joey confuses it with LaGuardia airport, Cecilia corrects him
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey geography confusion with efficient correction

jokes.text: And I just got what you meant. That is... That's a tricky one.
jokes.setup: Cecilia says Joey could 'own a few places down there'
jokes.punchline: Joey again takes it literally first, then realizes it's the confidence metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's literal interpretation pattern but less impact than the first time

tmdb_overview: Joey's soap opera character awakens with a transplanted brain. Ross practices bagpipes for the wedding. Phoebe and Rachel fight over someone's left cellphone.
